title: The Fall Map
description: Geographic world map of the WatchTheFall network. Geographic nodes are pinned at real lat/lng. Theme nodes live under the WTF home button.

nodes.label: WatchTheFall
nodes.thesis: The global hub watches the whole pattern: culture, power, technology, collapse, and renewal.

nodes.label: ScotlandWTF
nodes.thesis: Scotland asks whether a nation can recover meaning after institutions, identity, and civic trust have drifted apart.

nodes.label: BritainWTF
nodes.thesis: Britain is the old system trying to remember what it promised and who it was built to serve.

nodes.label: EnglandWTF
nodes.thesis: England is the centre that became invisible to itself while carrying the weight of everyone else's story.

nodes.label: WalesWTF
nodes.thesis: Wales holds the tension between cultural memory, economic neglect, and the need for a living future.

nodes.label: NorthernIrelandWTF
nodes.thesis: Northern Ireland exposes how unresolved history keeps shaping the present long after everyone claims to have moved on.

nodes.label: IrelandWTF
nodes.thesis: Ireland asks what independence means when economic, cultural, and institutional pressures arrive from everywhere at once.

nodes.label: EuropeWTF
nodes.thesis: Europe is the experiment in distance: power moving upward while ordinary life becomes harder to steer.

nodes.label: FranceWTF
nodes.thesis: France reveals what happens when civic ideals, street reality, and state authority pull against each other.

nodes.label: GermanyWTF
nodes.thesis: Germany tests whether order can survive when confidence, energy, industry, and identity lose their old alignment.

nodes.label: NetherlandsWTF
nodes.thesis: The Netherlands turns planning into a worldview, then asks what happens when people feel planned around.

nodes.label: PolandWTF
nodes.thesis: Poland carries the memory of pressure from both directions and turns survival into cultural instinct.

nodes.label: SwedenWTF
nodes.thesis: Sweden shows how high-trust societies break differently: quietly at first, then all at once.

nodes.label: SpainWTF
nodes.thesis: Spain tracks the gap between beauty, memory, youth pressure, and a state struggling to hold the centre.

nodes.label: ItalyWTF
nodes.thesis: Italy understands decline aesthetically before it understands it administratively.

nodes.label: TheWestWTF
nodes.thesis: The West is less a place than a shared argument about liberty, power, memory, and fatigue.

nodes.label: USAmericaWTF
nodes.thesis: America turns every crisis into a broadcast and every broadcast into a test of reality.

nodes.label: CanadaWTF
nodes.thesis: Canada asks how a soft national language can conceal hard administrative control.

nodes.label: AustraliaWTF
nodes.thesis: Australia shows what isolation, compliance, resource politics, and cultural distance do to a modern society.

nodes.label: AIWTF
nodes.thesis: AI does not only automate work. It reflects what humans have forgotten how to decide.

nodes.label: GadgetsWTF
nodes.thesis: Gadgets reveal a civilisation trying to buy novelty while forgetting what progress was for.

nodes.label: ComedyWTF
nodes.thesis: Comedy is what remains when official language can no longer carry reality honestly.

nodes.label: DarkHumourWTF
nodes.thesis: Dark humour survives where politeness fails because it admits the situation is already absurd.

nodes.label: ConceptsWTF
nodes.thesis: ConceptsWTF names the invisible structures: paradoxes, illusions, incentives, and mental traps.
